notes: Widely used in 1-2 processor workstations and servers; continues as Intel's primary server CPU brand
Multiprocessor variant for 4-32 processor servers; MP branding discontinued, capability folded into Xeon Scalable
64-bit EPIC architecture processor; Madison iteration released mid-2003; final Itanium 9700 shipped 2017, support ended 2021
Used with Canterwood 875P chipset for performance desktops and entry workstations
Double data rate SDRAM; DDR266/333/400 variants discussed; succeeded by DDR2
Second-generation DDR; debuted 2004 as DDR2-400; 50% less power than DDR400; succeeded by DDR3, DDR4, DDR5
Parallel I/O bus at 133 MHz, 1.06 GB/s; PCI-X 2.0 proposed 266/533 MHz; largely superseded by PCI Express
Serial I/O bus; PCIe 1.0 (2003) through PCIe 6.0 (2022) and PCIe 7.0 in development; paper predicted decade-long life, proved even longer
Hardware management interface; v1.5 current at study time; v2.0 released 2004; being superseded by DMTF Redfish
Low-latency data center interconnect; predicted to blossom with PCI-E; now dominant in HPC and AI clusters (NVIDIA acquisition of Mellanox)
Serial disk interface replacing parallel ATA; supported by Canterwood 875P chipset; still widely used for HDDs and some SSDs
AGP 8x offered 2.1 GB/s; paper predicted PCI Express would replace AGP for graphics, which occurred by 2006-2008
Dual GbE supported on Xeon servers; 10GbE discussed as emerging via PCI Express; GbE still ubiquitous
High-end Pentium 4 chipset; supported ECC, SATA, dual-channel DDR400 with PAT, GbE
Next-generation Xeon for 2004; debuted with higher clock speeds and EM64T (64-bit extensions)
Expected to be industry's first chipset with PCI Express and DDR2; shipped as Intel E7520/E7525
Next-generation 32-bit multiprocessor Xeon MP; expected by end of 2004
Four-processor chipset for Potomac; Intel's first 4-way IA-32 chipset since re-entering server market in 2002
Two-way Xeon server chipset; supports 16 GB memory, 3 PCI-X buses, dual GbE
Workstation chipset for Xeon; AGP 8x support, over 4 GB/s memory bandwidth
Itanium 2 chipset for 2-8 way servers; extensive RAS logic for mission-critical workloads
